what_it_does: Provides the inspector + Vi-rail + quick-adder companion components for the Registry canvas. TypeInspector slides in to inspect/edit one CV_REGISTRY Type (rename, edit description, duplicate, make variant, delete, edit slots and variables visually); SlotZones/SlotZone/AcceptsQuickEdit edit a Type's slots and what they accept; VariableTags edit template variables; ViChatScoped and ViStudioRail run scoped/global Vi conversations that propose registry updates or new Types; AIRegistryPanel projects window.CV_AI; QuickAdder is a modal that asks Vi to draft a new Type from a plain-English brief.
observations: All editing is done in place against window.CV_REGISTRY via R.update/register/remove/children/lineage/resolve, with subscribe-driven re-render (tick).
Built-in Types are read-only (isBuiltIn): name/desc inputs and slot/variable edits disabled; user is steered to Duplicate (fork) or + Variant (child extends parent).
duplicate() registers a copy with provenance:'user' and forkedFrom; makeVariant() registers a child with extends set to the parent id and provenance:'user'.
SlotZone shows live candidatesForSlot matches as thumbnails and an AcceptsQuickEdit that toggles accepted layers using R.LAYERS / R.LAYER_INFO (excludes 'template').

runViAction builds a structured prompt forcing the model to choose ONE of three JSON intents (update / create-variant / answer), resolves the 'claude' provider via CV_AI.resolveProvider, and throws loudly if CV_AI is absent.
Vi update/variant results are returned as PROPOSALS requiring an explicit Apply click before R.update/register run (gated application).
JSON parsing uses try/catch with a regex {…} fallback; an unparseable reply degrades to a 'try rewording' message (model-quality outcome, not swallowed error).
runStudioAction and QuickAdder delegate type drafting to window.CV_TYPES_VI.proposeType / suggestSlots; AIRegistryPanel groups CV_AI items by AI.LAYERS and shows the active surface.
AIRegistryPanel uses inline styles with CSS-var fallbacks (var(--accent-gold, #E0C010), var(--ink-2, #5b4628)) rather than pure tokens.
Exports all components via Object.assign(window, {...}) at the end.
standouts: Three-intent forced-JSON Vi action (update / create-variant / answer) with explicit brand voice instructions baked into the prompt, and provider resolution through CV_AI (loud-fail) per the project's AI-registry law.
Proposal-then-apply gating: Vi never mutates the registry directly; the user confirms each proposed update/variant.
Built-in protection: editing a built-in is impossible; the UI offers fork (duplicate) or extend (variant) instead — preserving the registry's inheritance model.
AIRegistryPanel makes the AI catalogue inspectable as a live projection of CV_AI alongside the type registry — same register/query/subscribe idea on one screen.
Visual slot editing with live candidate-match thumbnails (candidatesForSlot) makes composition tangible.
conventions: Strongly follows the project's non-negotiables: interface as a projection of the registries (subscribe + query), loud-fail on missing CV_AI, model access routed through CV_AI.resolveProvider rather than a raw endpoint.
Window-global registration and React-via-globals; rs-* class namespace for the workshop UI.
Provenance and lineage are first-class (forkedFrom, extends, lineage breadcrumbs).
AIRegistryPanel breaks the pure-token convention by using inline CSS-var-with-hex fallbacks instead of class/var-only styling.
concerns: runStudioAction comment 'For now: any open-ended brief becomes a proposeType call' signals a known stub/simplification — global Vi only ever proposes new Types.
AIRegistryPanel's inline hex fallbacks (#E0C010, #5b4628) are a second place those values appear, a drift risk the project explicitly warns against.
Several effects call R.subscribe / AI.subscribe with [] deps and return the unsubscribe; correct, but multiple components subscribing independently multiplies re-renders.
ViChatScoped.applyProposal compares x.proposal === p by reference to mark applied; works only because the same object is threaded through, fragile if proposals are cloned.
Variable/slot keys are free-text inputs with no uniqueness/validation, so duplicate keys are possible.
Many capabilities depend on window.CV_TYPES_VI being loaded; if absent, several actions silently no-op (return early) rather than failing loud.
notes: The full inspect-and-edit half of the workshop registry: in-place visual editing of one Type (slots, variables, lineage), proposal-gated Vi actions routed through CV_AI, built-in protection via fork/variant, plus a global Vi rail and a live CV_AI projection panel.
questions: What is the contract of window.CV_TYPES_VI (proposeType, suggestSlots) and where is it defined/seeded?
Is the 'For now' single-intent global studio action intended to grow into the same three-intent dispatch as the scoped chat?
Should AIRegistryPanel's inline hex fallbacks be removed in favor of pure tokens per the no-second-home rule?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the header comment (side-panel inspector + persistent Vi rail + quick adder, visual editing, Vi always present) accurately describes the implemented components, with the noted 'for now' simplification in the global studio action.
apparent_themes: Registry-as-truth with the UI as its live projection
AI as a proposal engine gated by explicit user apply
Inheritance discipline (built-in protection, fork vs variant, lineage)
Single-source AI access (CV_AI) with loud failure
intention_signals: Aims to make editing the type registry feel like direct, visual manipulation with Vi as an ever-present, proposal-making collaborator — while strictly preserving the registry's inheritance/provenance model and routing every model touch through one catalogue.
novelty: Combines visual slot/variable editing with live candidate-match thumbnails, a forced three-intent JSON Vi dispatch gated by apply, built-in fork/variant protection, and an inline live projection of the AI catalogue (CV_AI) next to the type registry.
contribution: Contributes the registry's editing surface: TypeInspector with in-place rename/desc/duplicate/variant/delete, visual SlotZones/VariableTags editors with candidate matching, scoped + global Vi proposal flows routed through CV_AI, and the AIRegistryPanel projection of CV_AI.
summary_for_embedding: React inspector/Vi-rail/quick-adder components for the CV_REGISTRY type registry: in-place visual editing of slots and variables, built-in fork/variant protection, proposal-gated Vi actions via CV_AI, and a live AIRegistryPanel projection of CV_AI.
intention_for_embedding: To let the user inspect and edit registry Types by direct visual manipulation with an ever-present Vi that proposes (never silently applies) updates and new Types, all routed through the single AI catalogue.
